items.title: Inside Shadow-Earth-053: A China-Aligned Cyberespionage Campaign Against Government and Defense Sectors in Asia
items.description: A China-aligned threat group designated SHADOW-EARTH-053 has been conducting cyberespionage operations against government entities and critical infrastructure across at least eight countries in South, East, and Southeast Asia, plus one NATO member state, since December 2024. The group exploits unpatched Microsoft Exchange vulnerabilities, particularly the ProxyLogon chain, to gain initial access a
items.ai_summary: The Shadow-Earth-053 threat group has been conducting cyberespionage operations against government entities and critical infrastructure in Asia, exploiting unpatched Microsoft Exchange vulnerabilities, particularly the ProxyLogon chain, to gain initial access. The group's attacks have been focused on gaining access to sensitive information, with potential real-world impacts including intellectual property theft and disruption of critical infrastructure. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should ensure that their Microsoft Exchange servers are updated to the latest version, specifically Exchange Server 2013 CU23 or later, Exchange Server 2016 CU22 or later, and Exchange Server 2019 CU11 or later. Additionally, defenders should apply the specific security updates for the ProxyLogon vulnerability, including KB5000871 and KB5000881, and configure their systems to use secure communication protocols, such as TLS 1.2 or later. Defenders should also monitor their systems for suspicious activity, including unusual login attempts and data transfers, to quickly detect and respond to potential attacks.

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: A stealthy RAT burrowing deep into Android devices
items.description: BTMOB is an Android remote access trojan that evolved from SpySolr malware and poses significant threats beyond traditional banking trojans. The malware combines phishing-led delivery with an APK builder interface that enables rapid payload generation without coding skills. Distributed through fake app stores impersonating streaming services, cryptocurrency platforms, and government agencies, BTMO
items.ai_summary: The BTMOB Android remote access trojan is a stealthy malware that has evolved from the SpySolr malware, posing significant threats to Android devices. It combines phishing-led delivery with an APK builder interface, allowing for rapid payload generation without requiring coding skills, and is distributed through fake app stores impersonating streaming services, cryptocurrency platforms, and government agencies. If exploited, this malware can lead to unauthorized access and control of Android devices, potentially resulting in sensitive data breaches and financial losses, affecting individuals and organizations alike. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust phishing detection and prevention measures, such as educating users about the risks of downloading apps from untrusted sources and utilizing mobile device management solutions to monitor and control app installations. Additionally, defenders should ensure that all Android devices are running the latest version of the operating system and have up-to-date security patches applied.

items.title: Reloaded in a modern Remcos RAT Infection
items.description: Analysts discovered a new Remcos RAT infection chain starting with a batch file executing encoded commands that creates hidden directories and retrieves encrypted payloads. Unlike earlier campaigns relying on PowerShell-hosted .NET loaders, this variant incorporates DonutLoader shellcode and AutoIt-based staging for in-memory payload delivery. The infection begins with a phishing email containing
items.ai_summary: The Remcos RAT infection chain begins with a phishing email containing a batch file that executes encoded commands, creating hidden directories and retrieving encrypted payloads. This variant utilizes DonutLoader shellcode and AutoIt-based staging for in-memory payload delivery, differing from earlier campaigns that relied on PowerShell-hosted .NET loaders. If exploited, this infection can lead to remote access and control of affected systems, with potential real-world impact including data breaches and ransomware attacks, particularly targeting individuals and organizations with weak email security. To remediate, defenders should implement email filters to block phishing emails, restrict AutoIt and batch file execution, and monitor for suspicious activity, such as unusual network connections or hidden directory creation. Additionally, defenders can update their security software to detect and block DonutLoader shellcode and AutoIt-based malware.

    C -->|T1027| D[Persistence]:::tactic

items.title: Operation Dragon Weave: Uncovering a China-Linked Campaign Targeting Czech Republic and Taiwan Using Azure Cloud C2
items.description: A sophisticated cyber-espionage campaign attributed to China-linked actors targets officials and citizens in Czech Republic and Taiwan through spearphishing attacks. The operation deploys malicious ZIP archives containing dual infection paths that ultimately deliver AZUREVEIL, an Adaptix C2 agent. The campaign uniquely leverages Microsoft Azure Blob Storage as a dead-drop command-and-control chann
items.ai_summary: The Operation Dragon Weave campaign, attributed to China-linked actors, utilizes spearphishing attacks to target officials and citizens in the Czech Republic and Taiwan. The attack vector involves sending malicious ZIP archives with dual infection paths, ultimately delivering the AZUREVEIL malware, an Adaptix C2 agent. If exploited, this campaign can lead to data encryption and exfiltration, with real-world impact including compromised sensitive information and potential disruption of government services. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering, ensure all systems are updated with the latest security patches, and monitor Azure Blob Storage activity for suspicious behavior. Specific remediation steps include configuring Azure Storage to use secure transfer protocols and enabling Azure Security Center for threat detection.

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Campaign Hits Red Hat Cloud Services npm Packages
items.description: A supply chain attack compromised multiple @redhat-cloud-services npm packages, executing malicious payloads automatically during installation via preinstall hooks. The attack uses AES-GCM encrypted payloads and obfuscated JavaScript loaders to harvest GitHub Actions secrets, npm tokens, cloud credentials (AWS, Azure, GCP), Kubernetes and Vault material, SSH keys, Git credentials, and cryptocurren
items.ai_summary: The Mini Shai-Hulud campaign is a supply chain attack that compromised multiple @redhat-cloud-services npm packages, executing malicious payloads during installation via preinstall hooks. The attack uses AES-GCM encrypted payloads and obfuscated JavaScript loaders to harvest sensitive information such as GitHub Actions secrets, npm tokens, cloud credentials, Kubernetes and Vault material, SSH keys, Git credentials, and cryptocurrency. The affected packages are part of Red Hat Cloud Services, and the attack vector involves the exploitation of the npm package installation process. To remediate this threat, defenders should remove the compromised packages and update to the latest versions, and also rotate and revoke any potentially exposed credentials, such as GitHub Actions secrets and cloud credentials. Additionally, defenders should monitor their cloud accounts and npm packages for suspicious activity and implement security controls such as multi-factor authentication and least privilege access.

    C -->|T1027| D[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    D -->|T1078.004| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: FSB’s matryoshka #1/3 – Gamaredon’s gifts that keeps unpacking – GammaPhish and GammaWorm
items.description: Gamaredon, a cyberespionage group operated by Russia's FSB, conducts long-term intrusion operations targeting Ukrainian government, military, and critical infrastructure. This analysis documents their 2026 infection chain, which uses HTML smuggling with weaponized xHTML files delivering RAR archives that exploit CVE-2025-8088 to extract HTA files into Windows Startup directories. The chain deploys
items.ai_summary: The Gamaredon group, operated by Russia's FSB, has been conducting long-term intrusion operations targeting Ukrainian government, military, and critical infrastructure entities. The infection chain utilizes HTML smuggling with weaponized xHTML files, delivering RAR archives that exploit a vulnerability to extract HTA files into Windows Startup directories. If exploited, this could lead to the deployment of GammaPhish and GammaWorm, resulting in data encryption and potential disruption of critical infrastructure. To remediate, defenders should implement strict email filters to block suspicious HTML attachments, ensure all systems are updated with the latest security patches, and monitor for unusual HTA file executions in the Windows Startup directory. Additionally, network traffic should be monitored for signs of data encryption and exfiltration, such as unusual RAR archive downloads or Mshta executions.

items.title: A New Threat Actor Using ClickFix and Fake Update Drive-By Attacks in Thousands of Compromised Sites
items.description: A sophisticated threat actor named DriveSurge operates as an Initial Access Broker using a Pay-Per-Install model to deliver malware at scale. The actor compromises thousands of legitimate websites and uses zTDS (Traffic Distribution System) to silently redirect visitors to malicious content. Victims encounter either FakeUpdates campaigns that impersonate browser update prompts for 11 different bro
items.ai_summary: The DriveSurge threat actor utilizes a Pay-Per-Install model to deliver malware at scale, compromising thousands of legitimate websites and leveraging zTDS to silently redirect visitors to malicious content. The attack vector involves drive-by compromise, where victims are redirected to fake update campaigns impersonating browser update prompts for 11 different browsers, potentially leading to the installation of malware. If exploited, victims may experience unauthorized access to their systems, data breaches, or ransomware attacks. To remediate, defenders should implement robust web application firewalls, ensure all software is up-to-date, and utilize browser extensions that detect and block malicious redirects. Additionally, network traffic monitoring and incident response plans should be in place to quickly respond to potential compromises.

items.title: Malicious npm packages abuse dependency confusion to profile developer environments
items.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence identified an active supply chain attack involving malicious npm packages that employ dependency confusion techniques. Between May 28-29, 2026, a threat actor using three maintainer aliases published malicious packages across nine organizational scopes that mirror real corporate namespaces. The packages execute obfuscated reconnaissance payloads through npm lifecycle
items.ai_summary: The malicious npm packages exploit dependency confusion to profile developer environments, specifically targeting organizations with similar namespaces. The attack involves three maintainer aliases publishing malicious packages across nine organizational scopes, executing obfuscated reconnaissance payloads through npm lifecycle hooks. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information, with real-world impact including potential data breaches and intellectual property theft. To remediate, developers should verify the authenticity of npm packages, ensuring they are installed from trusted sources, and monitor their environments for suspicious activity. Additionally, organizations should implement package validation and verification processes, such as using npm's built-in audit features or third-party tools like Snyk or npm-audit, to detect and prevent similar attacks.

items.title: Nimbus RAT: How Threat Actors Are Abusing Microsoft Teams and Google Drive to Deploy a Java RAT
items.description: In April 2026, threat actors deployed Nimbus RAT against a legal industry target using Microsoft Teams voice phishing. The attack began with email bombing (282 emails in 90 minutes), followed by a fake IT helpdesk contact via Teams who convinced the victim to grant Quick Assist remote access. Within 20 minutes, a Java-based RAT was deployed that uses Google Drive and Google Sheets for command-and-
items.ai_summary: The Nimbus RAT threat involves a sophisticated phishing campaign targeting the legal industry, where threat actors use Microsoft Teams voice phishing to trick victims into granting remote access via Quick Assist. The attack begins with a barrage of emails, followed by a fake IT helpdesk contact that convinces the victim to provide access, allowing the deployment of a Java-based RAT within 20 minutes. This RAT utilizes Google Drive and Google Sheets for command and control, highlighting the abuse of legitimate services for malicious purposes. To remediate, defenders should implement strict access controls for remote access tools, ensure multi-factor authentication for all services, and educate users about the risks of voice phishing and the importance of verifying the identity of IT support personnel. Additionally, monitoring for unusual activity related to Google Drive and Google Sheets can help in early detection of such threats.

items.title: CVE-2018-25347: WordPress Contact Form Maker Plugin 1.12.20 contains SQL injection vulnerabiliti...
items.description: WordPress Contact Form Maker Plugin 1.12.20 contains SQL injection vulnerabilities that allow authenticated attackers to manipulate database queries through the FormMakerSQLMapping and generete_csv_fmc AJAX actions. Attackers can inject malicious SQL code via the 'name' and 'search_labels' parameters to extract sensitive database information or escalate privileges.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2018-25347 is a SQL injection vulnerability in the WordPress Contact Form Maker Plugin version 1.12.20, allowing authenticated attackers to manipulate database queries through the FormMakerSQLMapping and generete_csv_fmc AJAX actions. The vulnerability can be exploited by injecting malicious SQL code via the 'name' and 'search_labels' parameters, potentially leading to sensitive database information extraction or privilege escalation. Real-world impact includes unauthorized access to sensitive data, with affected systems including WordPress installations using the vulnerable plugin version. To remediate, defenders should update the Contact Form Maker Plugin to version 1.12.21 or later, and restrict access to the AJAX actions to prevent exploitation. Additionally, defenders should monitor database logs for suspicious activity and consider implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent SQL injection attacks.

items.title: CVE-2018-25346: WordPress Form Maker Plugin 1.12.24 and below contains SQL injection vulnerabili...
items.description: WordPress Form Maker Plugin 1.12.24 and below contains SQL injection vulnerabilities that allow authenticated attackers to manipulate database queries by injecting SQL code through the FormMakerSQLMapping and generete_csv actions. Attackers can submit POST requests with malicious SQL payloads in the name and search_labels parameters to extract, modify, or escalate privileges within the WordPress d
items.ai_summary: The WordPress Form Maker Plugin version 1.12.24 and below contains SQL injection vulnerabilities, specifically CVE-2018-25346, which can be exploited by authenticated attackers to manipulate database queries. The vulnerability can be exploited through the FormMakerSQLMapping and generete_csv actions by submitting POST requests with malicious SQL payloads in the name and search_labels parameters. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to the extraction, modification, or escalation of privileges within the WordPress database, affecting all users of the plugin. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update the WordPress Form Maker Plugin to version 1.12.25 or later, and ensure that all instances of the plugin are configured to use prepared statements and parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection attacks. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious database activity and implement web application firewalls to detect and prevent malicious requests.

items.title: CVE-2018-25345: 10-Strike Network Scanner 3.0 contains a local buffer overflow vulnerability in ...
items.description: 10-Strike Network Scanner 3.0 contains a local buffer overflow vulnerability in the host name field that allows attackers to bypass SafeSEH protections and execute arbitrary code. Attackers can craft a malicious payload in the host name or address field and trigger the vulnerability through the Trace route or System information functions to achieve code execution.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2018-25345 is a local buffer overflow vulnerability in 10-Strike Network Scanner 3.0, specifically in the host name field, allowing attackers to bypass SafeSEH protections and execute arbitrary code. The vulnerability can be triggered through the Trace route or System information functions by crafting a malicious payload in the host name or address field. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to code execution, potentially allowing attackers to gain control over the affected system, with real-world impact including data breaches and lateral movement within a network. To remediate, defenders should update to a version of 10-Strike Network Scanner that is not vulnerable to this exploit, such as version 3.1 or later, and ensure that all system and network defenses are up to date. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious activity related to TTPs such as T1595, T1059, T1203, and T1082, which may indicate an active exploitation attempt.

items.title: CVE-2018-25344: 10-Strike Network Inventory Explorer 8.54 contains a stack-based buffer overflow...
items.description: 10-Strike Network Inventory Explorer 8.54 contains a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in the registration key input field that allows local attackers to execute arbitrary code by triggering a structured exception handler overwrite. Attackers can craft a malicious registration key string with 4188 bytes of padding followed by SEH chain values and shellcode, then paste it into the registrat
items.ai_summary: CVE-2018-25344 is a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in 10-Strike Network Inventory Explorer version 8.54, allowing local attackers to execute arbitrary code by exploiting the registration key input field. The attack vector involves crafting a malicious registration key string with 4188 bytes of padding followed by SEH chain values and shellcode, which can be pasted into the registration field to trigger the vulnerability. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to code execution, potentially allowing attackers to gain control over the affected system, with real-world impact including potential data breaches and lateral movement within a network. To remediate, defenders should update to a version of 10-Strike Network Inventory Explorer later than 8.54, and consider implementing input validation and sanitization for registration key fields. Additionally, defenders can monitor for suspicious activity, such as unusual registration key input, to detect potential exploitation attempts.

items.title: CVE-2018-25343: Smartshop 1 contains a cross-site request forgery vulnerability that allows atta...
items.description: Smartshop 1 contains a cross-site request forgery vulnerability that allows attackers to modify user profiles by tricking authenticated users into submitting malicious requests. Attackers can craft HTML forms targeting editprofile.php with hidden fields for email and password parameters that execute automatically when visited by an authenticated admin user.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2018-25343 is a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerability in Smartshop 1 that allows attackers to modify user profiles by tricking authenticated users into submitting malicious requests to editprofile.php. The attack vector involves crafting HTML forms with hidden fields for email and password parameters that execute automatically when visited by an authenticated admin user. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized modifications of user profiles, potentially affecting administrative users who have the ability to manage user accounts. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update Smartshop to a version that includes the patch for CVE-2018-25343 and implement CSRF protection measures, such as validating request headers and using tokens to verify request authenticity. Additionally, defenders should ensure that all user input is validated and sanitized to prevent malicious requests from being executed.

items.title: CVE-2018-25342: Smartshop 1 contains a time-based blind SQL injection vulnerability that allows ...
items.description: Smartshop 1 contains a time-based blind SQL injection vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to manipulate database queries by injecting SQL code through the 'searched' parameter in search.php. Attackers can send GET requests with malicious SQL payloads like SLEEP commands to extract sensitive database information including product details and system data.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2018-25342 is a time-based blind SQL injection vulnerability in Smartshop 1 that allows unauthenticated attackers to manipulate database queries through the 'searched' parameter in search.php. Attackers can exploit this vulnerability by sending GET requests with malicious SQL payloads, such as SLEEP commands, to extract sensitive database information, including product details and system data. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is significant, as it can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, potentially affecting customers and the organization's reputation. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update Smartshop to the latest version and ensure that input validation and sanitization are properly implemented, specifically by validating user input in the 'searched' parameter and limiting database query execution time. Additionally, defenders can configure their web application firewalls to detect and prevent SQL injection attacks, such as by implementing a rule to block requests containing suspicious SQL keywords.

items.title: Android Update Patches Exploited Zero-Day, 123 Other Vulnerabilities
items.description: Google says the Android vulnerability CVE-2025-48595 has been exploited in limited, targeted attacks. The post Android Update Patches Exploited Zero-Day, 123 Other Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Anthropic Expanding Mythos Access to 150 New Organizations
items.description: Only approximately 50 companies have had access to Mythos until now and they have found thousands of vulnerabilities in their products. The post Anthropic Expanding Mythos Access to 150 New Organizations appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Red Hat removes tainted packages after software pipeline compromise
items.description: According to the company’s preliminary analysis, a compromised GitHub account was used to push the malicious code out to customers, hitting 32 packages downloaded roughly 117,000 times a week.

items.title: CISA flags two-year-old Oracle flaw as actively exploited in attacks
items.description: CISA has ordered government agencies to secure their systems against a high-severity Oracle WebLogic Server vulnerability that was patched two years ago and is now actively exploited in attacks. [...]

items.title: The Zero-Knowledge Threat Actor and the End of Responsible Disclosure
items.description: AI can help attackers generate malware, create malicious payloads, bypass simple security checks, and convert vague malicious intent into functional code. The post The Zero-Knowledge Threat Actor and the End of Responsible Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical Vulnerability in HP VoIP Phones Enables Enterprise Network Breaches
items.description: A stack-based buffer overflow bug can be exploited for remote code execution on a vulnerable device. The post Critical Vulnerability in HP VoIP Phones Enables Enterprise Network Breaches appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Beyond Assume-Breach: How AI-Native Security Will Reshape Enterprise Defense
items.description: Twenty years after Dark Reading launched, we're looking ahead at what's next for enterprise security. Spoiler: It's hyper-segmented, AI-orchestrated, and way more sophisticated than your dad's firewall.

items.title: AI-Driven Exploitation is Destroying Vulnerability Management. Here’s How to Handle It.
items.description: AI-driven exploitation timelines are rapidly shrinking, and they are not going to stop shrinking. Vulnerabilities are being discovered, reproduced, and weaponized faster than ever in the history of enterprise security. As a result, the window between a vulnerability being disclosed and indiscriminate exploitation observed across the internet is now measured in hours, not days. The industry's

items.title: Oracle WebLogic Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
items.description: The vulnerability is CVE-2024-21182 and it can be exploited without authentication to hack affected WebLogic servers. The post Oracle WebLogic Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Google fixes one actively exploited Android zero-day, 124 flaws
items.description: Google has released the June 2026 Android security patches to address 124 vulnerabilities, including one zero-day flaw exploited in targeted attacks. [...]

items.title: Meta AI Hands Over High-Profile Instagram Accounts to Hackers
items.description: Exploiting a confused deputy weakness, the hackers simply asked the chatbot to link the account to a new email address. The post Meta AI Hands Over High-Profile Instagram Accounts to Hackers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: How Leading Organizations Are Turning EDR Into Operational Resilience
items.description: Most organizations now recognize that endpoint protection alone is no longer sufficient. That's why adoption of endpoint detection and response (EDR) has accelerated rapidly in recent years. Organizations understand that modern attacks move faster, evade traditional prevention controls, and require continuous visibility into suspicious activity across the environment. But owning EDR

items.title: Supply Chain Attack Hits 32 Red Hat NPM Packages
items.description: Hackers published 96 malicious package versions, injected with a credential-stealing worm similar to Mini Shai-Hulud. The post Supply Chain Attack Hits 32 Red Hat NPM Packages appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Pakistan-Linked SideCopy Targets Afghanistan Finance Ministry with Xeno RAT
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a spear-phishing campaign likely undertaken by the Pakistan-aligned SideCopy group targeting Afghanistan's Ministry of Finance with an open-source remote access trojan called Xeno RAT. "The campaign opens with a spear phishing delivery - a ZIP archive containing a malicious LNK file bearing a carefully crafted Pashto-language filename,"

items.title: Dashlane Brute-Force Attack Leads to Limited Encrypted Vault Downloads
items.description: Dashlane’s security systems automatically locked accounts to protect them against the hacking attempts. The post Dashlane Brute-Force Attack Leads to Limited Encrypted Vault Downloads appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New Wave Of Phishing Emails with SVG Files, (Tue, Jun 2nd)
items.description: For a few days, my SANS ISC mailbox is flooded with emails that delivers SVG files. An SVG ("Scalable Vector Graphic") is a web-friendly vector file format used for graphics and icons. No URL in the body, just â€œan imageâ€, that&#x27s the perfect way to deliver some malicious content. This isn&#x27t the first time that we see this technique used by threat actors[1].

items.title: Oracle’s First Monthly Patches Resolve 77 Vulnerabilities
items.description: Oracle’s monthly Critical Security Patch Update (CSPU) rollouts are meant to deliver critical fixes faster. The post Oracle’s First Monthly Patches Resolve 77 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Dashlane Discloses Brute-Force Attack, Encrypted Vaults of Fewer Than 20 Users Downloaded
items.description: Password manager Dashlane has disclosed that "fewer than" 20 users on the personal subscription plan had their encrypted vaults downloaded following a brute-force attack launched by an unknown party. On May 31, 2026, the company said an "external" threat actor launched a brute-force attack against certain Dashlane user accounts with the aim of breaking two-factor authentication (2FA)

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, June 2nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9954, (Tue, Jun 2nd)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Spain arrests suspected hacker for publishing personal data of police, prosecutors and cyber officials
items.description: Police described the incident as a large-scale disclosure of sensitive personal information that posed a threat to both the affected individuals and the institutions they serve. The data was allegedly posted on multiple internet platforms.

items.title: Hackers hijack thousands of sites for ClickFix and FakeUpdate attacks
items.description: A threat actor tracked as DriveSurge has been operating large-scale malware distribution campaigns using ClickFix and FakeUpdates techniques on compromised sites. [...]

items.title: Red Hat npm packages compromised to steal developer credentials
items.description: More than 30 npm packages under Red Hat's '@redhat-cloud-services' namespace were compromised in a supply-chain attack that distributed a new variant of the Shai-Hulud credential-stealing malware, dubbed "Miasma." [...]

items.title: Spain arrests doxer leaking sensitive data of govt employees
items.description: The Spanish National Police has arrested an individual for leaking sensitive information related to members of various key state organizations, including the National Cybersecurity Institute (INCIBE). [...]

items.title: Anthropic to Open Mythos AI to EU's ENISA
items.description: The European security agency's entry to Project Glasswing is the result of "strong bilateral cooperation" between the European Commission and Anthropic.

items.title: Inspector general finds NIST mistakes have made vulnerability database ineffective
items.description: NIST’s National Vulnerability Database (NVD) backlog mushroomed from 13,000 unprocessed security vulnerabilities in February 2024 to more than 27,000 by the end of 2025, “undermining the NVD’s utility and public trust," according to an inspector general report.

items.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
items.description: After a disgruntled security researcher published several zero-day exploits in recent weeks, Microsoft seemingly indicated criminal charges were in order.

items.title: NSA selects new leads for key cybersecurity posts
items.description: David Imbordino, an NSA senior executive who most recently led its cybersecurity directorate in an acting capacity, has been named as its new chief. Bruce Jones, a career NSA technical and operational leader, as the new head of its Cybersecurity Collaboration Center.

items.title: WP Maps Pro Vulnerability Exploited to Take Over WordPress Sites
items.description: The security defect (CVE-2026-8732) allows unauthenticated attackers to create administrative accounts on the affected installations. The post WP Maps Pro Vulnerability Exploited to Take Over WordPress Sites appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Dashlane password manager users locked out by brute force attacks
items.description: Multiple Dashlane users have been locked out of their accounts following brute-force attacks that attempted logins from distant locations and unknown devices. [...]

items.title: Miasma Supply Chain Attack Compromises Red Hat npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Worm
items.description: A new Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack campaign, codenamed Miasma, has compromised @redhat-cloud-services packages to steal credentials and secrets from developer machines and deliver a self-propagating worm. "This is effectively a Mini Shai-Hulud campaign: it uses the same core tactics of install-time execution, credential harvesting, CI/CD targeting, encrypted exfiltration, and potential

items.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.title: WordPress malware campaign hides payloads in Steam profiles
items.description: Nearly 2,000 WordPress websites were infected with malware that relies on Steam Community profile comments to hide command-and-control (C2) data. [...]

items.title: Microsoft investigates Office Apps, Teams file access issues
items.description: Microsoft says an ongoing incident is preventing users of its Teams collaboration platform and Office for the web cloud-based productivity suite from opening files. [...]

items.title: Patch Now: Another Palo Alto Auth Bypass Bug Under Active Exploit
items.description: Exploiting the PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN vulnerability requires certain conditions, but adversaries have done so in two attack waves that started in mid-May.

items.title: Race Against Time: Why Faster Vulnerability Alerts Matter
items.description: Attackers are exploiting vulnerabilities faster than many organizations can identify and patch them. SecAlerts explains why faster vulnerability alerts can help reduce exposure and improve response times. [...]

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: New Linux Flaw, PAN-OS Exploit, AI-Powered Attacks, OAuth Phishing and More
items.description: Monday hit like a cron job with anger issues. A busted auth path here, a repo-side faceplant there, some "patched-ish" thing already getting chewed on in the wild, and then the usual bonus round: poisoned dev tools, sketchy forum chatter, phishing kits pretending to be productivity, and AI lowering the bar for people who already thought 'curl | sh' had a personality. The vibe is simple: old

items.title: Critical Windows Netlogon RCE flaw now exploited in attacks
items.description: The Centre for Cybersecurity Belgium (CCB), the country's national authority for cybersecurity, warned on Friday that threat actors are now exploiting a recently patched critical Windows Netlogon vulnerability in attacks. [...]

items.title: Microsoft says it will not pursue security researchers after zero-day backlash
items.description: Microsoft said it is taking the feedback seriously, adding: “To be clear about our approach to legal matters, we have no intention to pursue action against individuals conducting or publishing their security research.”

items.title: China-Aligned Groups Ramp Up Attacks: Dragon Weave Hits Czech Republic & Taiwan
items.description: A new cyber espionage campaign codenamed Operation Dragon Weave has been observed targeting officials and citizens in the Czech Republic and Taiwan to deliver an AdaptixC2 agent. According to Seqrite Labs, targets of the campaign include government, research, academic, technology, and financial services sectors. The activity entails distributing spear-phishing emails containing ZIP attachments

items.title: The Security Growth Platform: Why MSPs Are Moving Beyond vCISO Tools
items.description: Three years ago, the practical question for an MSP building a cybersecurity practice was which "vCISO platform" to buy. The term was good shorthand for the work at the time: assessments, advisory, reporting, maybe a compliance module bolted on the side. The work has since outgrown the descriptor. A Security Growth Platform is the more precise name for what MSPs and MSSPs need from the software

items.title: OpenAI Codex Authentication Tokens Stolen in codexui-android npm Supply Chain Attack
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new malicious supply chain campaign that's targeting developers using OpenAI Codex through a legitimate-looking remote web UI. The tool, named codexui-android, is advertised on GitHub and npm as a remote web UI for OpenAI Codex, attracting over 29,000 weekly downloads. The package is still available for download from the repository. What

items.title: Critical WP Maps Pro Flaw Actively Exploited to Create Admin Accounts
items.description: Threat actors are attempting to actively exploit a critical security flaw impacting WP Maps Pro, a WordPress plugin that has had over 15,000 sales on the Envato Market, to create malicious administrator accounts on susceptible sites. WP Maps Pro allows site owners to embed customizable Google Maps and OpenStreetMap with markers, listings, and advanced location features on WordPress sites. It is

items.title: YARA-X 1.17.0 Release, (Sun, May 31st)
items.description: YARA-X&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s 1.17.0 release brings 5 improvements (several performance improvements) and 1 bugfix.

items.title: Name That Toon: Mark of (Cybersecurity) Progress
items.description: As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary package, we asked readers for a cybersecurity-related caption that captures their thoughts about the industry's last two decades.

items.title: As Global Powers Explore Humanoid Robots, Cyber-Risk Looms
items.description: The future of cybersecurity is germinating, as nation-states vie for dominance in the embodied AI market and its supply chain.

items.title: Asia's Cyber Insurance Market Shows Signs of Life
items.description: The cyber insurance industry has made relatively weak inroads into Asia due to a variety of factors, but that could be changing.

items.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
items.description: Researchers discover an exploit chain combining over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities that could have compromised a popular automation service.

items.title: 'The Com' Cyberattacks Support Violence & Sexploitation
items.description: Your organization's security failures have consequences for everyone else as well since this criminal gang uses its cyber winnings to support more violent and widespread crimes.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 29th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9950, (Fri, May 29th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Analysis of a Year of Files Uploaded to DShield Sensors, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: Using the data collected over the past year and using Kibana these two ES|QL query to summarize the data, this shows the list of the most uploaded threat to two DShield sensors (local and cloud) over the past year. I have sorted the activity by months that shows the evolution of files uploaded to the sensors each month. The activity peaked during the winter months (Dec 2025 - Feb 2026) and started decreasing in March 2026 for each sensor.

items.title: Dutch Raid Fails to Dent Russian Bulletproof Host
items.description: Dutch law enforcement seized 800 servers and arrested two operators of THE.Hosting but left the hosting provider's core IP address space intact.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, May 28th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9948, (Thu, May 28th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Reconstructing an Akira Ransomware Kill Chain from Perimeter and Endpoint Logs, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: Most Akira write-ups focus on the ransom note or the encryption routine. By the time those show up the interesting forensic work is over. The questions that matter to defenders sit earlier. How did they get in. When did they get domain admin. What did they touch before the binary fired. Those answers live in the days before impact. They sit in two log sources that almost never get joined. The perimeter firewall and the Windows event channel.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, May 27th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9946, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
